title: A method for cooperatively carrying a work piece
abstract: The disclosed embodiments relate to a method for cooperatively carrying a work piece by a plurality of mobile material handling robots, wherein each of the plurality of mobile material handling robots comprises a user endpoint for wireless communication. In one embodiment, a method is proposed, which includes the steps of gathering the plurality of mobile material handling robots around the workpiece, initiating a direct wireless communication amongst the user endpoints, setting up a working clock domain amongst the user endpoints for establishing time synchronization and a periodic deterministic communication amongst the user endpoints and carrying the work piece by the plurality of mobile material handling robots while exchanging control command messages and control feedback messages amongst the user end-points.
claims: Claims CLAIMS : 1. A method for cooperatively carrying a work piece by a plural ity of mobile material handling robots, wherein each of the plu rality of mobile material handling robots comprises a user end point for wireless communication, the method including the steps of : gathering the plurality of mobile material handling robots around the workpiece, initiating a direct wireless communica tion amongst the user endpoints; setting up a working clock domain amongst the user endpoints for establishing time synchronization and a periodic determin istic communication amongst the user endpoints; and; carrying the work piece by the plurality of mobile material handling robots while exchanging control command messages and control feedback messages amongst the user endpoints. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the direct wireless communication is initiated by a direct fifth generation or 5G communication . 3. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims, wherein the direct wireless communication is one or more of a D2D, a ProSe and a sidelink communication. 4. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims, wherein each user endpoint is a fifth generation or 5G user equipment or UE . 5. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims, wherein the working clock domain is effected by a time-sensitive network . 6. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims, wherein only authorized user endpoints are allowed to partici pate in the direct wireless communication. 7. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims, wherein one of the plurality of mobile material handling robots assumes a controller functionality and said one of the plurality of mobile material handling robots is controlling other collabo rating mobile material handling robots. 8. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims 1-6, wherein a distributed control is effected by the mobile material handling robots. 9. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims, wherein the direct wireless communication amongst the user end points is an assisted direct wireless communication including a coordination of resources by a base station. 10. The method according to claim 9, wherein the coordination of resources is handed over from a first base station to a second base station. 11. The method according to claim 10, wherein the second base station is a base station of a public mobile network. 12. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims 1- 8, wherein the direct wireless communication amongst the user endpoints is a non-assisted direct wireless communication in cluding a coordination of resources amongst user endpoints of the plurality of mobile material handling robots. 13. The method according claim 7, wherein the user endpoints of the mobile material handling robots set up communication ser vices between said mobile material handling robot assuming a controller functionality and collaborating mobile material han dling robots. 14. The method according claim 8, wherein the user endpoints of the mobile material handling robots set up communication ser vices amongst the plurality of mobile material handling robots. 15. The method according to one of the aforementioned claims, wherein a user endpoint belonging to an intermediate mobile ma terial handling robot bidirectionally relays control command messages and control feedback messages between distant mobile material handling robots.
